evidence_entries.evidence_text: Frames were returned as a chronological neighboring sequence around the requested timestamp or temporal anchor, including frames at 129.00s and 130.00s.
Additional chronological frames were retrieved from the same clip at 124.00s, 125.00s, 126.00s, 127.00s, and 128.00s.

The newly provided transcript/text-context evidence includes exact victory-oriented phrases and a celebration description: Transcript 1 states "Fourth time's the charm, and that's the one that matters most" and "we were great defensively second quarter"; Transcript 2 states "We had to beat really good teams to win" and "the kids stepped up and did that"; Transcript 3 states "We go 9-15, but we take third at state." The same evidence package also describes a women's basketball team celebrating on a stage, with a coach lifting a large wooden trophy, and confirms the team is in white jerseys.

The later interview frames at 129.00s and 130.00s do not show clear jerseys or a scoreboard; they show people in grey hoodies and one person in a black-and-white jacket. These frames do not determine the winner by themselves, but they no longer control the answer because earlier retrieved celebration evidence provides the needed team-color cue.

inference_steps.text: The newly grounded transcript evidence indicates a successful outcome rather than a cancellation, tie, or disqualification. In particular, the transcript says "Fourth time's the charm, and that's the one that matters most," "We had to beat really good teams to win," and "the kids stepped up and did that."

The transcript also states "We go 9-15, but we take third at state," which is a completed positive tournament result and is incompatible with the game being cancelled, tied, or ending in disqualification.

For the black-versus-white discriminator, the earlier celebration evidence is answer-critical: it describes a women's basketball team celebrating on a stage with a trophy lift and confirms that the celebrating team is in white jerseys.

The later interview frames at 129.00s and 130.00s do not show clear jerseys or a scoreboard, but that limitation does not overturn the earlier grounded celebration evidence. Since the winning/celebrating team is identified as wearing white jerseys, the supported option is that the white team won.

audit_report.feedback: The trace is aligned to the multiple-choice question and justifies a unique option: victory-oriented transcript phrases rule out cancellation/tie/disqualification, and the celebration evidence grounds that the winning team wore white jerseys.

diagnostics.temporal_alignment: pass: the trace distinguishes earlier celebration evidence (124-128s) from later interview frames (129-130s) and does not overuse the later frames.
diagnostics.referent_alignment: pass: the celebrating team described in white jerseys is treated as the winning team, consistent with the victory-oriented transcript and trophy celebration evidence.
diagnostics.evidence_sufficiency: sufficient from text alone: transcript phrases support a completed successful result, and the celebration/text-context evidence supplies the black-vs-white discriminator.

transcript_segments.text: Fourth time's the charm, and that's the one that matters most, doesn't it? Yeah, I mean, they're an unbelievable team. They're very good, and mentally for us to kind of get over that hurdle against Campbell and show up the way we did today, getting down early in the first quarter and the resolve that we had in the second quarter, we were great defensively second quarter and gave ourselves a chance in the second half, so just very proud of the girls. What was going wrong there early when you were down 14-3?

We just were flustered a little bit, and we didn't settle in yet, and kind of had butterflies, I think, and we weren't making reads on our offense. We were kind of guessing on stuff, but the girls settled in and bought into what our game plan was, and we had a lot of kids step up. Defensively, again, we were great in the second quarter, but they were doubling Mesa, and we kind of recognized that, and we had kids make shots when they were open. You know, Avery shot the lights out today. Cam hit big ones. It just was a team effort. We had to have everybody we had them.

transcript_segments.text: You had that hot finish there the second quarter. How did you try to make sure that you didn't have the big letdown coming out of halftime? Well, we just talked at half about how we got through that quarter because we were really good defensively. We got after them a little bit, and our defense turned into offense. And we tried to carry that through the second half. And Campbell came in, and they made a lot of shots. And Cammie Curtis finishes with dang near 30, I think. But we limited everybody else. We were great on the basketball. It was our defense that carried us through the game. Obviously, we shot the ball well, but we had to get stops, and we got them.

As much as nobody likes to lose, was maybe losing that regional championship game kind of a blessing in disguise considering how the state bracket was shaped and how you went through it? We had to beat really good teams to win, and we knew that regardless. So whoever we got in the first round, second round, third round, we knew we were going to have to beat good teams, and the kids stepped up and did that.

considering three years ago this team only won nine games? Did he make state tournament two years ago as well and fell short in third place last year? How have you seen this program kind of turn around? Well, Addy and Allie inherited a program with two wins the year before. They come in and they work their tails off that summer and

transcript_segments.text: We go 9-15, but we take third at state. We win big games down the stretch. They kind of got a feel for that, but they never stopped working. Adeline and Allie came in and shot for the last four weeks, five weeks they came in and shot and made 700 shots a week in the morning. They don't know how to stop working, and they had a goal, and they continue to work towards that goal, and they deserve everything. I'm very proud of them.

segments.text: Fourth time's the charm, and that's the one that matters most, doesn't it? Yeah, I mean, they're an unbelievable team. They're very good, and mentally for us to kind of get over that hurdle against Campbell and show up the way we did today, getting down early in the first quarter and the resolve that we had in the second quarter, we were great defensively second quarter and gave ourselves a chance in the second half, so just very proud of the girls. What was going wrong there early when you were down 14-3?

We just were flustered a little bit, and we didn't settle in yet, and kind of had butterflies, I think, and we weren't making reads on our offense. We were kind of guessing on stuff, but the girls settled in and bought into what our game plan was, and we had a lot of kids step up. Defensively, again, we were great in the second quarter, but they were doubling Mesa, and we kind of recognized that, and we had kids make shots when they were open. You know, Avery shot the lights out today. Cam hit big ones. It just was a team effort. We had to have everybody we had them.

You had that hot finish there the second quarter. How did you try to make sure that you didn't have the big letdown coming out of halftime? Well, we just talked at half about how we got through that quarter because we were really good defensively. We got after them a little bit, and our defense turned into offense. And we tried to carry that through the second half. And Campbell came in, and they made a lot of shots. And Cammie Curtis finishes with dang near 30, I think. But we limited everybody else. We were great on the basketball. It was our defense that carried us through the game. Obviously, we shot the ball well, but we had to get stops, and we got them.

As much as nobody likes to lose, was maybe losing that regional championship game kind of a blessing in disguise considering how the state bracket was shaped and how you went through it? We had to beat really good teams to win, and we knew that regardless. So whoever we got in the first round, second round, third round, we knew we were going to have to beat good teams, and the kids stepped up and did that.

considering three years ago this team only won nine games? Did he make state tournament two years ago as well and fell short in third place last year? How have you seen this program kind of turn around? Well, Addy and Allie inherited a program with two wins the year before. They come in and they work their tails off that summer and

We go 9-15, but we take third at state. We win big games down the stretch. They kind of got a feel for that, but they never stopped working. Adeline and Allie came in and shot for the last four weeks, five weeks they came in and shot and made 700 shots a week in the morning. They don't know how to stop working, and they had a goal, and they continue to work towards that goal, and they deserve everything. I'm very proud of them.

captions.audio: acoustics: 1) Tone of speech: N/A. 2) Background sounds or music: Loud, celebratory cheering and clapping from the team and coaches.
attributes: camera_state: The camera is at a medium distance, capturing the entire team and a large portion of the coaching staff on the stage. The shot is static.
video_background: The team is on a dark stage, with a black curtain behind them. The lighting is focused on the group.
storyline: The scene transitions to the women's basketball team's victory celebration, establishing their achievement.
shooting_style: This is a wide shot capturing the group celebration.

transcript_spans.text: We go 9-15, but we take third at state. We win big games down the stretch. They kind of got a feel for that, but they never stopped working. Adeline and Allie came in and shot for the last four weeks, five weeks they came in and shot and made 700 shots a week in the morning. They don't know how to stop working, and they had a goal, and they continue to work towards that goal, and they deserve everything. I'm very proud of them.
